what_it_does: Renders a fixed 1600x900 presentation slide with a centered title, two embossed metric boxes, a horizontal hatched stat band, two engine panels flanking a central SVG system diagram, subtle blueprint underlays, a thin footer hatch, and a circular V mark.
observations: The layout is entirely file-local HTML and CSS except for a Google Font import and /surface/styles/vars.css.
The composition uses a fixed .view canvas centered in the browser body.
The upper area contains two large embossed stat boxes for development time and IP investment.
The middle stat band displays six compact metrics over an ochre diagonal hatch.
The lower content area uses two warm/cool engine plates around a central SVG diagram showing Engine One, Engine Two, version labels, dashed connectors, and a hub icon.
A late v2.1 correction overrides the footer band into a thin quiet hatch and hides its original icon and label.
The visual language depends heavily on CSS custom properties for colors, spacing, radii, and type sizes.
standouts: Layered metric presentation: embossed boxes above a separate hatched stat band.
Central SVG geometry acts as the bridge between the two engine plates.
The file preserves footer markup while visually suppressing it through override CSS.
conventions: Uses absolute positioning for the main slide regions.
Uses CSS variables with dna-style names for spacing, type, and radii.
Uses inline comments to label design groups and corrections.
Uses inline SVG for the central brand/system geometry.
Uses !important overrides for the footer correction rather than removing earlier rules or markup.
concerns: Fixed 1600x900 dimensions provide no responsive behavior inside this file.
The footer band contains hidden copy and icon markup after the v2.1 override.
The final footer override relies on multiple !important declarations.
The file depends on external CSS variables; without /surface/styles/vars.css many styles will not resolve.
notes: A fixed presentation composition for a scale/proof slide, built around metrics, two-engine framing, and central brand geometry.
questions: Should the hidden footer copy remain in the DOM after the v2.1 correction?
Are the stat values intended as static presentation text or placeholders fed from elsewhere?
Should the slide ever adapt to dimensions other than 1600x900?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated title matches the file: it implements embossed boxes, a stat band, and brand/system geometry in a v2 slide.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to communicate credibility and scalability by pairing numeric proof with a two-engine system model and controlled brand-like visual structure.
novelty: The distinct feature is the combination of embossed metric blocks, a hatched horizontal stat strip, overlapping engine plates, and a central SVG connector diagram within one fixed slide.
contribution: Contributes a metric-led slide substrate with separated proof tiers, two-engine explanatory framing, branded central geometry, blueprint corner texture, and a quiet corrected footer treatment that a fused system could retain as compositional ingredients.
summary_for_embedding: A fixed 1600x900 HTML/CSS presentation slide showing proof metrics, a hatched stat band, two engine panels, and a central SVG system diagram with brand geometry.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for presenting scale, investment, adoption, and a two-engine product model in a polished static design variation.
